title: geum urbanum subvar. opulifolium
intro: Geum urbanum subvar. opulifolium, also known as the wood avens or purple avens, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the rose family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Geum urbanum subvar. opulifolium include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
